title: OpenAPI 3.2, Compatibility, Deployment, and Production Defense · Stakeholder Go Mission
summary: An engineering, security, accessibility, data, client, and operations review board. The board needs layered test and fuzz evidence, race and load gates, observable health and incidents, an accurate OpenAPI 3.2 contract, compatibility policy, safe migrations, canary and drain evidence, rollback, and a defended production runbook. Planned artifact: Deliver Production Go API and Incident Defense with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners prog…
objectives: Run race detection, concurrency schedules, leak checks, load profiles, resource limits, and failure injection while separating correctness from capacity claims.
Apply current OpenTelemetry server span kind, low-cardinality route, method, status, error.type, network and server attributes, propagation, sampling, and 4xx semantics.
Use benchmarks, pprof in a protected plane, traces, saturation, tail latency, allocation, contention, load shape, and changed-fault evidence for capacity and incidents.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Commit to the mission model · Project Race Leak Load Test
steps.instruction:
project case — A project team handles case 1965 in a partner API with an outdated generated client. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate one OpenAPI-compatible, expand-first, canary, drain, and rollback release; then change the tenant and show deny-by-default before repository access. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Run race detection, concurrency schedules, leak checks, load profiles, resource limits, and failure injection while separating correctness from capacity claims.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Go server Commit to the mission model · Project Race Leak Load Test.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for run race detection, concurrency schedules, leak checks, load profiles, resource limits, and failure injection while separating correctness from capacity claims. For Go server Commit to the mission model · Project Race Leak Load Test, keep Race Leak Load Test connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.
